title: Any authenticated user can escalate to system_admin by PATCHing their own user_profiles row (account & tenant takeover)
fixApproach: Add an explicit WITH CHECK to the update policy that freezes privileged columns, e.g. WITH CHECK (id = auth.uid() AND role = (select role from public.user_profiles where id = auth.uid()) AND company_id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM (select company_id from public.user_profiles where id = auth.uid())); OR, more robustly, add a BEFORE UPDATE trigger on user_profiles that rejects any change to role/company_id unless the session is service_role, and narrow the authenticated UPDATE grant to a column list excluding role/company_id. Claude can write the migration + fold into schema.sql (FABLE); OWNER must apply it to live AND immediately audit `select id,email,role,company_id from user_profiles where role in ('system_admin','executive')` for any already-escalated accounts.
scoreImpact: Blocks go-live; single most severe issue in the RLS surface.

title: tickets UPDATE RLS policy lets an assigned supplier self-complete their own ticket (workflow + separation-of-duties bypass)
lines: schema.sql:1728-1730; app_supplier_ids schema.sql:1184-1192; user-client INSERT evidence app/api/tickets/route.ts:85
fixApproach: Since no user-scoped client updates tickets, drop the browser UPDATE policy entirely (or restrict USING to app_is_company_wide() only) so the service-role admin client remains the sole writer; if any browser UPDATE is ever needed, add a WITH CHECK that pins status/quote_value/completed_at to their old values (or a trigger that forbids status changes outside SECURITY DEFINER). Author as a migration for the human to apply to live.
scoreImpact: Critical production blocker — direct authorization bypass of the completion/sign-off control.

title: GET /api/suppliers returns every tenant's supplier directory (missing company_id filter)
fixApproach: Make requireAdmin() select `role, company_id` and return the companyId (as [id]/route.ts already does); in GET add `.eq('company_id', ctx.companyId)`. Consider adding a rateLimit to GET as defense-in-depth. Optionally enforce a DB-side company_id NOT NULL so the admin path can't silently drop the tenant tag.
scoreImpact: Cross-tenant data exposure — treat as launch blocker for multi-tenant confidentiality.

title: Store manager (client) can tamper with supplier quote amount/status — quotes UPDATE has no WITH CHECK
fixApproach: Restrict the quotes UPDATE policy to the supplier who owns the quote (supplier_id IN app_supplier_ids()) or admin roles, and add an explicit WITH CHECK; route client accept/decline through a server API that only flips status. Also tighten `quotes write` (INSERT, 1528-1530) so only suppliers/admins can insert quotes, not any ticket-visible store user.

title: GET /api/suppliers leaks every tenant's supplier directory (no company_id filter, RLS bypassed)
fixApproach: Either delete the dead route file (the UI page already redirects away) or scope it: capture company_id in requireAdmin() and add `.eq('company_id', companyId)` to the GET. Given suppliers[].company_id can be NULL for Motiv-pool rows, decide explicitly whether pool suppliers should be visible.
scoreImpact: Confirmed cross-tenant PII disclosure — production blocker for go-live.

title: signoffs (FOR ALL), quotes (UPDATE) and ticket_variations (FOR ALL) writable directly by see-ticket users — forged completions & quote-amount tampering
lines: signoffs schema.sql:1585-1588; quotes update schema.sql:1524-1526; ticket_variations schema.sql:1702-1705; app_can_see_ticket schema.sql:1196-1209
fixApproach: Drop the browser INSERT/UPDATE/ALL write policies on quotes, signoffs and ticket_variations (keep the SELECT policies), leaving the service-role admin client as sole writer; or add strict WITH CHECK/role predicates. Ship as a migration.
scoreImpact: High — record and financial tampering surface.

title: Losing/closed competitor supplier keeps write access to another supplier's awarded ticket (hasAccess ignores invite status)
fixApproach: In hasAccess(), gate the ticket_suppliers fallback on status IN ('invited','quoted') AND restrict it to commercial-phase statuses; for execution/close-out statuses require mine.includes(ticket.supplier_id). Alternatively resolve access per-action (quoting actions allow active invitees; execution actions require the awarded supplier).
scoreImpact: Broken object-level authorization between competing supplier tenants on shared tickets.

title: supplierId / supplierIds written to ticket without validating the supplier belongs to the ticket's company or region (cross-tenant assignment)
fixApproach: Before writing supplier_id / inserting ticket_suppliers, look up each supplier id and assert it is company-scoped to ticket.company_id or a shared pool supplier; reject unknown/out-of-scope ids with 400/403.
scoreImpact: Cross-tenant data exposure via unvalidated supplier assignment.

title: POST /api/suppliers creates supplier rows with NULL company_id (no tenant tag on write)
fixApproach: Have requireAdmin() return companyId and add `company_id: ctx.companyId` to the insert (mirroring provision's add_supplier at provision/route.ts:189 which correctly sets company_id).

title: POST /api/suppliers/bulk imports up to 500 suppliers with NULL company_id
fixApproach: Resolve the caller's company_id (select it in the auth check) and set `company_id` on each mapped row before insert.

title: Lower-privilege company members can forge approvals / decision_items / signoffs / snags — FOR ALL policies weaken WITH CHECK to company_id only
fixApproach: Make each WITH CHECK mirror its USING predicate (company + role/region/can_see_ticket). One migration updating all four policies; fold into schema.sql.

title: No role gate on supplier_escalations / supplier_invites / ticket_variations — any company member can CRUD; invite tokens exposed company-wide
fixApproach: Add `AND app_role() = ANY(ARRAY['system_admin','regional_manager', ...])` to USING and WITH CHECK on all three write policies, and scope supplier_invites read to admin roles (or drop token from client-visible reads). One migration; fold into schema.sql.

title: ticket_updates insert does not bind author_id/author_role to the caller — comment/audit impersonation
fixApproach: Add `AND author_id = auth.uid()` to the WITH CHECK (and validate author_role server-side, or drop it in favor of deriving role from the profile). Same treatment worth applying to ticket_evidence.uploaded_by (schema.sql:1674-1676).

title: app_settings is gated by role only, with no tenant scope — cross-tenant admin overwrite
fixApproach: Decide the model: either keep app_settings platform-global and restrict writes to a dedicated platform-owner (a specific user id / a super-admin flag) rather than any system_admin, or add company_id and make settings per-tenant. Since all reads/writes already go through the service-role admin routes, the direct-table policy is defence-in-depth — tighten it accordingly.

title: POST /api/suppliers and /api/suppliers/bulk create suppliers with no company_id (untenanted writes)
fixApproach: Delete these dead routes, or capture the caller's company_id in requireAdmin() and stamp `company_id: companyId` on every inserted row (rejecting supplier-role users with a null company_id).

title: Ticket supplier assignment/invite never validates the supplier_id belongs to the ticket's company (or Motiv pool)
fixApproach: Before writing supplier_id / inserting ticket_suppliers, load the suppliers row(s) and assert `company_id === ticket.company_id` OR the row is a verified Motiv-pool supplier (is_motiv/company_id null per the pool rules). Reject otherwise. Apply the same guard in transition (validate/require_assessment/request_quote/assign_snag), assign, and regional/tickets.

title: transition-engine submit_quote accepts Infinity / over-cap amounts (weaker than parseAmount)
fixApproach: Replace the inline Number()/>0 check in transition submit_quote with parseAmount() and return the same 400 on null.

title: transition approve_quote diverges from /quote-decision: leaves invites open, never sets supplier_id, marks ALL pending quotes accepted
fixApproach: Either remove approve_quote from lib/workflow + the transition switch, or make it award a single quote and close the other ticket_suppliers invites exactly like /quote-decision; and add a source-status guard to /submit-quote so quotes can't be submitted once the ticket has left the commercial phase.

title: tickets.assigned_user_id, asset_id, technician_id have no foreign keys
fixApproach: Add FKs in a migration: tickets.asset_id→assets(id), tickets.technician_id→technicians(id), tickets.assigned_user_id→user_profiles(id), each ON DELETE SET NULL (nullable cols), plus a supporting index on each; verify no existing orphan rows first (SELECT ... WHERE asset_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM assets)).

title: ratings table missing FKs on company_id, supplier_id, contractor_id, rated_by
fixApproach: Add FKs ratings.company_id→companies(id), ratings.supplier_id→suppliers(id), ratings.rated_by→user_profiles(id); decide contractor_id's referent (technicians?) and add FK or drop the column; add indexes on supplier_id and company_id. Backfill/clean orphans first.

title: technicians table has zero foreign keys (company_id, supplier_id unenforced)
fixApproach: Add technicians.supplier_id→suppliers(id) ON DELETE CASCADE and technicians.company_id→companies(id); index both. Clean orphans first.

title: No CHECK constraints on enum-like text columns (status/priority/type/severity/author_role) — typos persist silently
fixApproach: Because the live value set is broad and drifts, prefer adding CHECK constraints from the authoritative app enums (or reference lookup tables) rather than a narrow list; at minimum add ratings.score CHECK (score BETWEEN 1 AND 5) and author_role CHECK (author_role IN ('regional_manager','supplier')). Enumerate live DISTINCT values before constraining status columns to avoid rejecting valid rows.

title: FK / hot-path indexes not present in schema.sql and likely absent (tickets by company_id/store_id/region_id/supplier_id/status, quotes.ticket_id, notifications(user_id,read))
fixApproach: OWNER: run `select indexname, indexdef from pg_indexes where schemaname='public' and tablename in ('tickets','quotes','notifications')` on live and paste back. FABLE: add any missing btree indexes — tickets(company_id), tickets(store_id), tickets(region_id), tickets(supplier_id), tickets(status), quotes(ticket_id), notifications(user_id, read) — and fold them into schema.sql.

title: FKs are almost entirely NO ACTION; user-account erasure chain is effectively blocked
lines: 1044 (notifications.ticket_id set null), 1043 (ticket_events cascade), 1054 (user_profiles→auth.users cascade), 945/955/1049 (children of user_profiles NO ACTION)
fixApproach: Decide the deletion policy explicitly: keep soft-delete (active/closed_at) as the norm and document that hard-delete is unsupported, OR add an erasure routine that anonymises user_profiles-referencing rows (set actor/created_by to a tombstone) before delete. Set audit/actor FKs to ON DELETE SET NULL so history survives user removal.

title: WhatsApp webhook writes caller PII (phone numbers + free-text ticket content) to server logs
fixApproach: Drop the phone number and `extracted` content from these logs; log only non-identifying shape (message.type, a hashed/last-4 phone token, session id). Keep the existing safe log at L519.

title: /view omits tenant (company) match and has no rate limit — cross-company writes into ticket_views audit table
fixApproach: After loading the ticket, reject when ticket.company_id !== prof.company_id (or reuse the ticket-access helper), and add a rateLimit('view:{user.id}',…) guard.

title: /seen omits tenant match and has no rate limit — cross-company writes into ticket_reads
fixApproach: Add a ticket-access/company check before the upsert and a rateLimit guard.

title: Supplier self-signup onboarding reveals whether an email is already registered (user enumeration)
fixApproach: Drop the friendly pre-check (createUser already returns a generic 'account already exists' at 159-163) or return an identical generic message for both branches; optionally add a per-email rate limit.

title: supplier/assign-rm clear-path deletes ALL regional_user links for the store's region
fixApproach: Scope the delete to the specific RM (add `.eq('user_id', <currentRmId>)`) or redesign the store↔RM assignment to not mutate region-wide regional_users; at minimum confirm the region-wide intent.

title: Private-bucket storage upload policies are unscoped (bucket + logged-in only)
fixApproach: Add a name/path prefix predicate (e.g. `(storage.foldername(name))[1] = auth.uid()::text`) and role checks where appropriate, or explicitly document that these are intentionally inert and keep /api/uploads as the enforced path.

title: schedule action lets a supplier set technician_id to any technician UUID (no roster ownership check)
fixApproach: When technicianId is provided, look up the technician and confirm its supplier_id is one of the acting supplier's supplier_ids before writing; otherwise reject.

title: Supplier add_update flips ticket status open→in_progress outside the workflow engine
fixApproach: Remove the inline status flip; add_update should only touch last_supplier_update_at and the update row.

title: ticket_suppliers has no unique(ticket_id,supplier_id); assign route doesn't de-duplicate supplierIds (duplicate supplier assignment)
fixApproach: Add unique(ticket_id, supplier_id) to ticket_suppliers (idempotent migration) and de-dup supplierIds in the assign route.

title: Evidence can be uploaded after close-out (no workflow-state gate on add_evidence / ticket_evidence)
fixApproach: Reject add_evidence when ticket.status is terminal (completed/cancelled/declined); optionally add the same guard in the ticket_evidence RLS check.

title: Additional child columns without FKs (ticket_suppliers.quote_id/company_id, daily_briefings.company_id, supplier_sla_acceptances.user_id, supplier_verification_docs.uploaded_by, ticket_events.company_id, store_ticket_counters.store_id)
fixApproach: Add the corresponding FKs (quote_id→quotes, company_id→companies, user_id/uploaded_by→user_profiles, store_id→stores). Keep uploaded_by/user_id ON DELETE SET NULL where the audit row should survive the user.

title: Multi-write workflows are non-atomic (ticket insert + notification fan-out, project milestone + file) — partial writes possible
fixApproach: For sequences that must be atomic, move the multi-write into a single SECURITY DEFINER Postgres function (RPC) so it runs in one transaction; for best-effort fan-out (notifications), keep sequential but wrap in try/catch and never let a notification failure surface as a ticket-create error. Audit which routes chain a status/ticket write with a dependent child insert.

title: schema.sql explicitly does not capture CHECK constraints or secondary indexes — integrity review of those is unverifiable against live
fixApproach: OWNER: run `pg_dump --schema-only` (or the existing supabase/diagnostics/export_live_schema.sql) and share. FABLE: reconcile CHECKs + indexes into schema.sql so the mirror is authoritative, and update the header disclaimer once captured.

title: Handled API 500s never reach Sentry; no log drain → weak error observability
fixApproach: Add `Sentry.captureException(e)` inside the catch blocks that return 500 (or a small `reportServerError()` helper used everywhere), and/or configure a Vercel log drain for retention + alerting.

title: Archived-notification purge lives only in the unscheduled v3-recompute cron → notifications table grows unbounded
fixApproach: Fold the archived-notification purge delete into the already-scheduled v3-snapshots handler (cheapest, no extra cron slot), or add v3-recompute to vercel.json crons.
scoreImpact: Slow free-tier DB exhaustion over time.

title: xlsx (SheetJS) pinned to a CDN tarball is invisible to npm audit + Dependabot, and parses untrusted uploads
fixApproach: Add an explicit scheduled reminder/check that compares the pinned xlsx version against SheetJS releases/advisories (e.g. a small CI step or calendared owner task), and keep the pin current. Confirm both parse routes are auth-gated and size-limited.
scoreImpact: Supply-chain monitoring gap on an untrusted-input parser.

title: Four moderate npm advisories via next's transitive postcss; non-blocking in CI
fixApproach: Track the next release that bumps postcss and take the Dependabot bump when available; no code change needed now.

title: Unauthenticated /api/csp-report forwards arbitrary POST bodies to Sentry at up to 60/min globally
fixApproach: Key the rate limit per client IP (and/or lower the global cap well under the Sentry budget), and avoid stuffing the raw report object into Sentry `extra` (log only the whitelisted directive/blocked/doc fields).

title: Executive role (documented read-only) is granted ticket write actions across its company
lines: workflow TRANSITIONS include 'executive' on many actions (89-176); assign 36; quote-decision 39
fixApproach: Decide the intended executive capability; either remove 'executive' from the write transition/role arrays and the assign/quote-decision role checks, or update the documentation to reflect intentional company-scoped write access.
scoreImpact: Role over-privilege vs documented read-only intent (company-scoped).

title: sla_rules read policy exposes global (company_id IS NULL) rows to anonymous users
fixApproach: Add `auth.role() = 'authenticated'` (or `auth.uid() IS NOT NULL`) to the NULL branch of the sla read policy.

title: Deny-all tables and SECURITY DEFINER functions reviewed — no defects found (confirmation)
fixApproach: Optional hardening only: also revoke EXECUTE on log_ticket_event() and archive_ticket_notifications() from anon/authenticated to match the other trigger functions.

title: CONTROL CONFIRMED: project progress is server-computed and not client-settable
lines: schema.sql:1871-1876 (generated column) + 1943-1950 (RLS admin-only write); stores/[storeId]/route.ts:36-66; guard.ts:15-26; progress.ts:46-63
fixApproach: No action required. (Optional nicety: milestones can be marked in any order, but since % is a count×25 this does not misstate progress.)

title: CONTROL CONFIRMED: separation of duties holds in the workflow engine (supplier cannot sign off own work or resolve own dispute)
lines: workflow.ts:141-171 (approve/close_out roles); dispute/route.ts:248-253 (confirm requires other party), :224-233 (withdraw/retract role-gated); signoff/route.ts:18 (RM-only)
fixApproach: No action on the engine; remediate via the RLS findings.
scoreImpact: None — positive assurance (conditional on fixing RLS).

title: audit_logs / ticket_events are immutable to end users, but there is no DB-level append-only/tamper-evidence guard
fixApproach: Accept as-is for now (end-user immutability is met). If tamper-evidence is desired later, add a BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE trigger raising an exception on audit_logs/ticket_events, or a periodic hash-chain. No change required to ship.

title: Secrets hygiene, security headers, keystore, and service worker checks passed
files: next.config.mjs; proxy.ts; .gitignore; android/app/build.gradle; android/keystore.properties.example; public/sw.js; app/manifest.webmanifest/route.ts
fixApproach: No action required; retain these controls.
